data.title: How to Create a Legally-Binding Will and Estate Plan Without Hiring a Lawyer for Less Than the Price of a New iPad
data.description:
For many people, estate planning sits firmly in the category of "important, but someday." It's easy to put off writing a will or setting up a trust when life is busy and the process feels intimidating, expensive, or overly legalistic. But as more Americans focus on building wealth, planning for retirement, and protecting their families, that mindset is starting to change.

WillMaker is an online platform designed to help individuals create legally valid estate planning documents including wills, living trusts, and powers of attorney from home, without needing to hire a lawyer. In addition to the online experience, users can also download the WillMaker software to work offline, a flexibility that sets it apart from many web-only competitors. The idea isn't to replace thoughtful planning, but to remove the friction that keeps people from getting started at all.

For households focused on long-term financial security, estate planning is a natural extension of investing and retirement planning. A portfolio, a home, or a small business only tells part of the story. The other part is ensuring that those assets transfer smoothly and according to your wishes. Without a will or trust in place, state laws decide who inherits what, which can lead to delays, higher costs, and unnecessary stress.

WillMaker aims to simplify that process. The platform walks users through step-by-step questions in plain language, helping them build documents that reflect their specific circumstances. You don't need legal expertise to get started. The software handles the structure while you focus on decisions like beneficiaries, guardianship, health care preferences, and financial authority.

One of WillMaker's biggest appeals is cost. Traditional estate planning attorneys can easily charge thousands of dollars for a basic will and trust package. WillMaker's bundles start at $109, less than the price of a new iPad (from $349 at the time of writing), making it a far more accessible option for individuals and families who want legally sound documents without a heavy upfront expense. For many users, that lower barrier is the difference between having a plan in place and having nothing at all.

The documents themselves are built on attorney-reviewed templates and updated to reflect current state laws. That legal backbone is critical. Estate planning isn't just paperwork, it's about ensuring your wishes hold up when they matter most. WillMaker's templates cover a wide range of needs, from simple wills to shared living trusts for couples, durable powers of attorney for finances, and health care directives that outline medical decisions if you're unable to speak for yourself.

The platform also goes beyond core estate documents. Users can create transfer-on-death deeds to pass real estate directly to beneficiaries without probate, draft final arrangement instructions to ease the burden on family members, and prepare letters to survivors that explain personal decisions in your own words. These details may not feel urgent, but they often make the biggest difference during emotionally difficult moments.

For older adults and retirees, WillMaker can be a way to organize years of financial planning into a clear, legally binding plan. For younger families, it provides peace of mind that children, assets, and health care wishes are protected if the unexpected happens. And for anyone building net worth over time, it ensures that progress isn't undone by avoidable legal complications.

Estate planning doesn't have to be complicated or intimidating to be effective. Quicken WillMaker offers a practical, affordable way to take control of that process, turning "someday" into something that's actually done.

For investors and planners who already think about the future, estate planning isn't a separate task. It's part of the same long-term strategy.

data.title: Gold Nears $4,700, Pushing These 2 Mining Financers Into 'Overvalued' Territory: Value Scores Sink
data.description:
As Gold surges toward the psychological $4,700 mark, hitting an all-time high of $4,640.13, two major royalty and streaming companies are flashing warning signs.

Gold Rally Creates Valuation Paradox

While Franco-Nevada Corp. (NYSE:FNV) and Osisko Gold Royalties (NYSE:OR) are riding the precious metal's momentum, their stock prices have risen so sharply that they are now considered significantly overvalued relative to their fundamentals.

According to Benzinga Edge data, the “Value” scores for both companies—which measure a stock’s worth by comparing market price to assets, earnings, and sales—have collapsed into the bottom 10th percentile this week.

Value Rankings Plunge Below 10th Percentile

The week-on-week data highlights a rapid deterioration in valuation metrics as investors pile into these stocks:

  • Franco-Nevada Corp.: The stock's Value score dropped from 11.27 to 9.40. Additional performance details, as per Benzinga’s Edge Stock Rankings, are available here.
  • Osisko Gold Royalties: The drop was even steeper, falling from 10.59 to a scarce 8.81. Additional performance details are available here.

These scores indicate that while the companies are financially robust, their current share prices may be outpacing their underlying financial measures like sales and operating performance.

High Momentum, Low Value

The drop in value scores is ironically a symptom of the stocks’ success. Both companies currently boast massive “Momentum” scores—93.72 for Osisko and 91.97 for Franco-Nevada. Additionally, both stocks show positive price trends across short, medium, and long timeframes.

Shares of FNV have advanced 49.92% over the last six months, 87.66% over the year, and 13.70% in 2026, so far. The stock was 0.89% higher in premarket on Wednesday.

Meanwhile, OR has gained 49.14% over the last six months, 118.58% over the year, and 14.36% YTD. OR was 0.88% higher in premarket on Wednesday.

Disclaimer: This content was partially produced with the help of AI tools and was reviewed and published by Benzinga editors.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: EXCLUSIVE: Micro-Cap ENvue Medical Launches OTC Reusable Syringes For Feeding, Medication Delivery
data.description:
ENvue Medical, Inc. (NASDAQ:FEED) on Wednesday said it launched a line of over-the-counter ENFit Syringes designed to meet both feeding and medication delivery clinical needs.

"With the launch of our over-the-counter ENFit Syringes, available in institutional and consumer-friendly packaging, we are expanding access to safe, affordable, and standardized enteral delivery tools…," said Doron Besser, CEO of ENvue Medical.

ENvue's Syringe Launch

ENvue's ENFit Syringes are currently anticipated to be available at certain online retailers, including Amazon and Durable Medical Equipment suppliers, in 2.5 mL, 5 mL, 10 mL, and 60 mL sizes, without the need for a prescription.

The ENFit syringes are designed for reuse for up to seven days or 20 uses, helping support safe and consistent enteral delivery in both clinical and home care environments.

Background

In December 2025, NanoVibronix Inc. changed its corporate name to ENvue Medical to better reflect its new strategic direction and primary focus on its ENvue feeding-tube placement system.

"Our strategy is now focused on scaling hospital utilization, strengthening our commercial footprint, and building a comprehensive enteral-feeding ecosystem around ENvue through both internal development and external business development opportunities," Besser said in a press release in December 2025.

Last week, ENvue Medical launched a new development and integration program leveraging vibration-based technology from its NanoVibronix division to address pain and discomfort associated with indwelling nasogastric tubes.

Nasogastric tubes are among the most widely used devices in hospital care and are frequently required to remain in place for extended periods. While clinically essential, indwelling NG tubes are commonly associated with nasal and throat discomfort during the dwell phase.

FEED Price Action: ENvue Medical shares were down 2.16% at $2.27 during premarket trading on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Glaukos, Trip.com Group And Other Big Stocks Moving Lower In Wednesday's Pre-Market Session
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were lower this morning, with the Dow futures falling around 100 points on Wednesday.

Shares of Glaukos Corp (NYSE:GKOS) fell sharply in pre-market trading after the company reported preliminary fourth-quarter and FY25 sales results.

Glaukos said it sees preliminary FY25 sales of $507 million.

Glaukos shares dipped 12.1% to $96.97 in pre-market trading.

Here are some other stocks moving lower in pre-market trading.

  • Briacell Therapeutics Corp (NASDAQ:BCTX) fell 53.1% to $5.12 in pre-market trading after the company announced pricing of $30 million public offering.
  • Trip.com Group Ltd (NASDAQ:TCOM) dipped 9.3% to $68.55 in pre-market trading. Trip.com Group confirmed earlier report that it is under investigation by China’s SAMR for potential anti-monopoly violations.
  • Pearson PLC (NYSE:PSO) shares fell 7.2% to $13.36 in pre-market trading after the company issued a trading update for 2025.
  • Olema Pharmaceuticals Inc (NASDAQ:OLMA) declined 5.5% to $26.37 in pre-market trading after falling 3% on Tuesday.
  • Fresenius Medical Care AG (NYSE:FMS) fell 4.8% to $21.79 in pre-market trading.
  • Ambitions Enterprise Management Co LLC (NASDAQ:AHMA) fell 4.3% to $11.81 in pre-market trading after jumping 134% on Tuesday.
  • NovaBay Pharmaceuticals Inc (NYSE:NBY) declined 3.8% to $12.55 in pre-market trading following a 3% drop on Tuesday.
  • SmartRent Inc (NYSE:SMRT) fell 2.3% to $1.74 in pre-market trading.
